This article treats the [[Qonto system/Legends|Qonto system]] as the location for one such homeworld.</ref>

{{Quote|So, how goes the Rebellion?|Lina Andonais|<ref name="RotM">[[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']]</ref>}}
The '''Qonto''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="CSWE" /> from the [[Qonto system/Legends|Qonto system]],<ref name="TEAOC" /><ref name="Planetnamia" /> which was located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Although generally not known for their perception, some exceptional Qonto had senses considerably sharper than the [[Human/Legends|Human]] standard.<ref name="CSWE" /> During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Lina Adonais]] was a Qonto space urchin who worked aboard the [[Kuari Princess/Legends|''Kuari Princess'']], a giant [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]], while she traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. She considered joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], and while on a tour of the [[Relgim Run/Legends|Relgim Run]], she encountered a group of Rebels disguised as [[Anomid/Legends|Anomids]]. She immediately saw through their disguise and questioned them about what they were doing aboard the ''Princess''. Adonais was also present on the cruise when it was [[Attack on the Kuari Princess|attacked]] by [[Riders of the Maelstrom|pirates]] while passing through [[Maelstrom Nebula/Legends|the Maelstrom]].<ref name="RotM" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Qonto were created by [[Ray Winninger]] for the [[roleplaying game]] adventure [[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']], which was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1989]]<ref name="RotM" /> and reprinted in [[1996]] as part of ''[[Classic Adventures: Volume Three]]''.<ref name="RotM2">[[Riders of the Maelstrom (adventure)|''Riders of the Maelstrom'']]</ref>
